title: War and Peace
author: Tolstoy, Leo, graf
summary: July 1805 St. Petersburg: Anna Pávlovna Schérer, a forty-year-old maid of honor to the Dowager Empress Márya Fëdorovna, holds a fashionable reception and decries Napoleon as the Antichrist. She and Prince Vasíli Kurágin exchange polished courtly banter about politics, alliances, and diplomatic dispatches, debating Austria, Prussia, England, and the Emperor Alexander’s lofty vocation. Society intrudes—fêtes, invitations, émigrés, and appointments like the proposed Baron Funke’s secretaryship at Vienna. Guests expected include the Vicomte de Mortemart and the Abbé Morio. The chapter contrasts theatrical social ritual with weighty geopolitical anxieties, revealing characters’ vanities, ambitions, and the era’s tense, uncertain mood and intrigue.
